title: Aina-i-Amraz-un-Nisa
gloss: A small pamphlet on the diseases of women.
notes: Sanyasi Ashram free-distribution medical pamphleteering at 10,000 copies (pair with s25, same day, same press).

author: Fateh Chand, Hakim
title: Sahih aur Sachcha Barqi Ilaj
gloss: A small tract on the treatment of certain diseases by electricity.
publisher: Lala Ishar Das, Manager, Suraj Prakash Medical Hall
copyright: Hakim Fateh Chand, Amritsar; No. 17—, dated the 31st October, 1910.
notes: 'The Correct and True Electric Cure' — electrotherapy advertising tract, free, 5,000 copies, yet copyrighted; medical hall marketing through the registry.

flags.issue: register No. partially cut at page edge (17—)

author: Ghulam Jilani Khan, Khan Sahib, Shams-ul-Atibba
title: Makhzan-i-Hikmat
gloss: A book of family medicine combining the oriental and the occidental systems with a vocabulary of medical terms.
copyright: Khan Sahib Dr. Ghulam Jilani Khan, Lahore; No. 160, dated the 25th October, 1910.
notes: 1,272-page hybrid Yunani/Western family-medicine compendium at 5,000 copies with dual binding prices — the largest medical book of the year in our extractions; author titled both Khan Sahib and Shams-ul-Atibba ('Sun of Physicians').

title: Hamdard-i-Nau Jawanan
gloss: Subject noticed in entry No. 17 at page 17 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th September, 1909.
notes: 'Friend of Young Men' — Sanyasi Ashram anti-onanism/sexual-hygiene tract, 10,000 free copies, 3rd edition; cross-reference 1909Q3 entry 17, page 17.

author: Ilm-ud-din, Hakim
title: Ma'dan-i-Hikmat
gloss: A book on Yunani medicine in simple Urdu in two parts.

author: Ram Kishan, Hakim
title: Buti Prakash
gloss: An illustrated book on Vaidic medicine.
notes: Illustrated Ayurvedic herbal by the same Hakim Ram Kishan writing fiction at p20 s35. First digit of copies smudged; read as 1,000.

author: Sant Singh, Master
title: Sarab-i-Sharab
gloss: A small tract describing the injurious effects of drinking.
notes: 'The Mirage of Liquor' — temperance filed under Medicine this time; banker-financed from Peshawar. First digit of copies smudged; read as 1,000.

author: Bhola Khan, Hakim
title: Jauhar-i-Adam almaruf Sheo Prakash
gloss: This book contains some alleged remedies for the cure of diseases, principles of the Yoga philosophy, methods of dream reading, &c.
notes: Muslim hakim publishing 'Essence of Adam, known as Sheo Prakash' — remedies + yoga + oneiromancy under a double Muslim/Hindu title; registrar's skepticism ('alleged remedies') on record. Urdu—Misc serials resume at 152 from Q3.
